description: Verifiable attestation that a buyer DID acquired a specific digital artifact from a seller DID. The seller writes this record into their own AT Proto repo; their repo signature constitutes the attestation. A purchase record is not self-asserted by the buyer.

main.description: A seller-issued acquisition record binding a buyer DID to an artifact proof. Must be stored in the seller's repo. The proofRef MUST use a strong ref (uri + cid) to pin the artifact identity at time of sale — preventing retroactive substitution.

proofRef.description: Immutable strong reference (AT URI + CID) to the at.ofwork.proof record identifying the artifact being sold. The CID component pins the proof at its exact version — it cannot be silently updated after the sale.

buyerDid.description: DID of the entity acquiring the artifact. Need not be an AT Proto DID — any resolvable DID method is acceptable (did:web, did:key, etc.).

sellerDid.description: DID of the entity conducting the sale. MUST match the DID of the AT Proto repo in which this record is stored, establishing the seller's cryptographic commitment to the transaction.

price.description: Denomination and amount paid. Use amount '0' for complimentary transfers.

transactionId.description: Optional opaque external payment reference. Examples: Stripe charge ID (ch_...), Lightning Network payment hash, on-chain transaction hash (0x...), PayPal transaction ID. Enables off-chain payment verification.

platform.description: Optional URI identifying the marketplace, platform, or application through which the sale was conducted. Provides provenance for the transaction context.

note.description: Optional seller-authored note about the transaction (e.g. edition number, batch reference, fulfilment details). Not a legal document.

purchasedAt.description: ISO 8601 datetime at which the transaction was completed. Should reflect the time of payment confirmation, not record creation.

price.description: A monetary amount in a specified currency or token.

amount.description: Decimal string representation of the amount to avoid floating-point precision loss. Examples: '9.99', '0', '1500.00', '0.000042'. Negative values are not permitted.

currency.description: ISO 4217 fiat currency code (USD, EUR, GBP) or a token identifier for digital assets (USDC, ETH, BTC). Case-insensitive for fiat; case-sensitive for tokens.
